sections.title: 2. Hosting and Server Operation
content: Our website is operated on a server administered by the law firm and hosted on the infrastructure of the hosting provider Hostinger.
The hosting provider only provides the technical infrastructure required to operate the server.
Any processing of personal data occurs solely within the framework of providing and ensuring the technical operation of the website.

sections.title: 3. Collection of Data When Visiting the Website
content: When you access our website, information is automatically transmitted by your browser to our website server and temporarily stored in log files.
This may include the following data:
IP address of the requesting device
Name and URL of the retrieved file
The data is processed to ensure the stable and secure operation of the website and to identify technical errors.

sections.title: 4. Contact Form
content: If you use our contact form, the information you enter will be processed.
The data will only be used to process your request and to contact you.
Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR (pre-contractual measures or contract performance).

sections.title: 5. Survey Form
content: Our website may provide a form for participating in surveys or providing additional information.
The data you provide may include:
Information about your situation or inquiry
The data helps us better understand inquiries and improve our legal services.
Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR (consent) or Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR if related to a legal inquiry.

sections.title: 6. Data Sharing
content: Personal data will generally not be transferred to third parties.
It is required to establish or perform a legal mandate
It is required to assert, exercise, or defend legal claims
As a law firm, we are also subject to statutory professional confidentiality obligations.

sections.title: 7. Cookies and Future Analytics
content: Currently, our website does not use tracking or analytics tools.
In the future, web analytics tools may be implemented to better understand the use of our website and improve our services.
If such tools are implemented, this privacy policy will be updated and users will be informed accordingly.

sections.title: 8. Your Rights
content: Under the GDPR you have the following rights:
Access to your stored personal data (Art. 15 GDPR)
You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.
State Commissioner for Data Protection and Freedom of Information North Rhine-Westphalia

sections.title: 9. Data Security
content: This website uses SSL/TLS encryption to ensure secure data transmission.
We also implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your data against manipulation, loss, destruction, or unauthorized access.

sections.title: 10. Updates to This Privacy Policy
content: This privacy policy is currently valid as of March 2026.
We reserve the right to amend this policy if legal requirements or technical changes to our website require it.
